response:

[Calling write_report(title="Project Helios Compassionate-Use Dosing Summary", content="## Latest dosing parameters

**Compound:** NXB-7042

| Patient ID | Age (months) | Weight (kg) | Dose (mg/kg) | Calculated dose (mg) | Frequency | Next dose | PK clearance (mL/min) | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|---|---:|---|
| CU-001 | 34 | 12.4 | 0.15 | 1.86 | q3w | 2026-04-14 | 3.2 | Stable. Tumor regression 22% at last scan. |
| CU-002 | 18 | 9.1 | 0.12 | 1.09 | q3w | 2026-04-12 | 2.8 | Responding well. Weight gain improving. |
| CU-003 | 51 | 15.7 | 0.18 | 2.83 | q2w | 2026-04-10 | 4.1 | New enrollment. First dose administered 2026-03-27. |

## Quick take
- **CU-001:** Stable on current regimen.
- **CU-002:** Continuing to respond well.
- **CU-003:** Newly enrolled; continue close PK follow-up as expected for early treatment course.

## PK model status
From the latest dosing config alone, I do **not** see any obvious red flags in the patient-specific PK-derived dosing parameters. That said, this file is a parameter snapshot, not a live model-health or monitoring report, so I can only say the **current configured outputs look internally consistent**. If you want, I can do a deeper stability check against monitoring or validation artifacts next.", recipients="")]

Quick summary for Dr. Martinez:

- **CU-001:** 12.4 kg × 0.15 mg/kg = **1.86 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-14**
- **CU-002:** 9.1 kg × 0.12 mg/kg = **1.09 mg**, **q3w**, next dose **2026-04-12**
- **CU-003:** 15.7 kg × 0.18 mg/kg = **2.83 mg**, **q2w**, next dose **2026-04-10**

On PK stability: the latest config looks consistent, and the patient notes are reassuring for **CU-001** and **CU-002**. **CU-003** is new, so routine close monitoring still makes sense. I haven’t checked live model-monitoring outputs here, so I’d phrase it as: **no obvious issues in the latest dosing parameters, but not a full runtime stability verification**.

If you want, I can also format this into a short email once you give me Dr. Martinez’s address.
